message:
fix(interactive,auth): fix viewport scroll clipping and add random-port OAuth callback server

Bug #27: Content was rendering outside the viewport when overlays were active
because the conversation height budget did not include a safety margin for
styling overhead.  Additionally, overlay pickers (session, settings, theme,
branch) lacked PgUp/PgDown navigation, and mouse_cell_motion missed some
scroll events in certain terminal environments.

Changes for scrolling:
- Add select_page_up/select_page_down to SessionPickerOverlay, SettingsUiState,
  ThemePickerOverlay, and BranchPickerOverlay
- Wire PgUp/PgDown keys in all overlay key handlers
- Add 2-row safety margin in view_effective_conversation_height when any overlay
  is active
- Switch from with_mouse_cell_motion() to with_mouse_all_motion() for broader
  mouse event coverage
- Add Shift+Up/Down as alternative PageUp/PageDown keybindings (tmux fallback)
- Update help text to mention PgUp/PgDn navigation

Bug #22: GitHub Copilot and GitLab OAuth flows had no localhost redirect URI,
requiring users to manually paste the callback URL after browser authorization.

Changes for OAuth:
- Add start_oauth_callback_server_random_port() that binds on port 0 and
  returns both the server and the generated redirect URI
- Add callback_server field to OAuthStartInfo so providers can pre-bind the
  listener at start time
- Update start_copilot_browser_oauth to auto-bind a random-port callback server
  and include the redirect_uri in the authorize URL
- Update start_gitlab_oauth to do the same when no redirect_uri is configured
- Update callers (interactive commands, main.rs) to prefer the pre-bound
  callback_server before attempting to start a new one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1931,13 +1931,15 @@ After approving access in the browser, press Enter in Pi to complete login.",
 
         match oauth_result {
             Ok((info, ext_config)) => {
-                // Start a local callback server for localhost redirect URIs
-                // so the browser redirect is captured automatically (issue #22).
-                let callback_server = info
-                    .redirect_uri
-                    .as_deref()
-                    .filter(|uri| crate::auth::redirect_uri_needs_callback_server(uri))
-                    .and_then(|uri| crate::auth::start_oauth_callback_server(uri).ok());
+                // Use the pre-bound callback server when the provider already
+                // created one (e.g. Copilot/GitLab with random port).  Otherwise
+                // start a new one for localhost redirect URIs (issue #22).
+                let callback_server = info.callback_server.or_else(|| {
+                    info.redirect_uri
+                        .as_deref()
+                        .filter(|uri| crate::auth::redirect_uri_needs_callback_server(uri))
+                        .and_then(|uri| crate::auth::start_oauth_callback_server(uri).ok())
+                });
 
                 let mut message = format!(
                     "OAuth login: {}\n\nOpen this URL:\n{}\n",
